mission.title: rook: Scrubbing is preventing cluster updates
mission.description: Scrubbing is preventing cluster updates. Requested by 14+ users.

steps.title: Check current rook deployment
steps.description:
Verify your rook version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
helm list -n rook 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working rook installation.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
**Is this a bug report or feature request?**
* Bug Report

**Deviation from expected behavior:**
Changing the cluster CRD doesn't cause changes to the cluster with error:

```
2018-10-25 1945.693175 I | op-osd: checking if any nodes were

steps.title: Apply the fix for Scrubbing is preventing cluster updates
steps.description:
The Ceph cluster should be considered clean for orchestration if the PGs are in the following states:
- `active+clean`
- `active+clean+scrubbing`
- `active+clean+scrubbing+deep`
```yaml
2018-10-25 19:05:45.693175 I | op-osd: checking if any nodes were removed
2018-10-25 19:05:45.768366 I | op-osd: adding node k8s-nvme-01.sdsc.optiputer.net to the removed nodes list
2018-10-25 19:05:45.768384 I | op-osd: processing 1 removed nodes
2018-10-25 19:05:45.768387 I | op-osd: processing removed node k8s-nvme-01.sdsc.optiputer.net
2018-10-25 19:05:45.768540 I | exec: Running command: ceph status --cluster=rook --conf=/var/lib/rook/rook/rook.config --keyring=/var/lib/rook/rook/client.admin.keyring --format json --out-file /tmp/679034901
2018-10-25 19:05:45.983434 W | op-osd: skipping t
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Scrubbing is preventing cluster updates" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
The Ceph cluster should be considered clean for orchestration if the PGs are in the following states:
- `active+clean`
- `active+clean+scrubbing`
- `active+clean+scrubbing+deep`

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with rook installed or the issue environment reproducible.
